category_title: Preschoolers with frequent inappropriate social behaviors (yelling, taking toys, crying)
minimal_context: Gregory is participating in free play time with his peers in the preschool classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for free play. Go pick a center and play with the other children.

STUDENT: (Walks over to a peer) I want those! (Takes the other child's toys)

TUTOR: Let's try to play together.

STUDENT: (Hides the toys behind his back) No!

TUTOR: Remember to use your words.

STUDENT: (Yells at the other child and starts crying) They are mine!

options.A: Make oneself available without forcing interaction, first simply sitting near Gregory, then speaking quietly and gently touching his shoulder.
options.B: Act persistently without becoming angry or insulting him in public, ensuring Gregory receives neither an irritated response nor an accusation.
options.C: Establish a norm where disagreement is acceptable and provide a platform for an evidence-based exchange of viewpoints during free play.
options.D: Fade reinforcement to a special choice board activity just on Fridays when Gregory has a thumbs-up week.
